vol_name: Puppet task training and reliability materials
vol_description:
This volume includes videos of the puppet task compiled for reliability training. Children are aged between 6 and 18 months. This dataset is compiled to be shared with other research groups who are going to use this task.
This volume is accessible to authorized databrary users only. When you open the files as an authorized user you have the option to download materials to run the task, code the data, and run the reliability analysis. As per databrary rules please ensure that video sessions are kept confidential and password protected.

This volume includes documentation for the CAFE Consortium's Media Assessment Questionnaire (MAQ). The MAQ is designed to capture demographic and household characteristics and the amount, type, content, context of media use in families with young children. The MAQ is the result of ongoing collaboration among an international group of CAFE Consortium Key Investigators. MAQ V2.4 includes measures validated by the CAFE consortium, the MEMO (Suh et al., 2025) measure of parent media motivations and JMES (Koch et al., 2024) of joint media engagement. The MAQ is supplemented by several other questionnaires including the Macarthur-Bates Communicative Development Inventory (Fenson et al., 2000), the Parent Stress Scale (Berry & Jones, 1995), and the DISRUPT measure of parent distraction with mobile devices (McDaniel, 2021).

To see the MAQ documentation including instrument description, please click the link below.

MAQ 2.4 is translated into several languages in addition to English [base language]:
	•	Brazilian Portuguese [In progress] 
	•	Chinese (Simplified) 
	•	Croatian [Partial] 
	•	French 
	•	French (Canada) 
	•	German 
	•	Italian 
	•	Polish [Complete except for in progress MCDI and MEMO short form]
	•	Spanish (US)
Includes materials that describe the data access process and the details of the variables and data integration process for the MAQ version 1 &2..
